corpus.title: No chance Netanyahu will be arrested in New York, Trump says, as mayor Mamdani explores options
corpus.signal_text:
No chance Netanyahu will be arrested in New York, Trump says, as mayor Mamdani explores options
Zohran Mamdani said his administration is in ‘active conversation’ about whether city authorities could arrest Netanyahu when he goes to a UN meeting in New York in September
President Donald Trump said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “will not be arrested” in the U.S., rebuffing New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s suggestion that such an apprehension could happen.
Mamdani has said his administration is in “active conversation” about whether city authorities could arrest Netanyahu when he comes to New York in September for the United Nations General Assembly. The Israeli leader has been charged with war crimes by the International Criminal Court.
“Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America,” Trump posted Monday on social media. “The only ones that should be arrested are the people that led Iran into this unprecedented SPIRAL OF DEATH AND DESTRUCTION.”
The back-and-forth marks the latest spat between Trump and Mamdani. The two men enjoyed a brief detente after Mamdani was elected last fall, but most recently, the president has labeled as “communists” candidates the mayor backed in Democratic primary elections.
Mamdani said in a New York Times interview published Saturday that Netanyahu “belongs in the Hague” and reiterated that his office is speaking with the city’s legal department about what steps it could take if the Israeli prime minister appears in New York.
Speaking at a Monday press conference, Mamdani accused Netanyahu of being “the architect of the genocide” of Palestinians in Gaza but has also made clear that “my administration will follow all applicable local laws.”
Israel has rejected accusations it has committed genocide against Palestinians, saying that its war in Gaza was a legitimate defence of its people and a justified response to the Oct. 7, 2023 attack by Hamas, which is considered a terrorist group by the U.S. and European Union. Militants stormed into Israel and killed around 1,200 people and took roughly 250 hostage.
Israeli military operations have, in turn, levelled much of Gaza and left more than 73,000 people dead, according to the territory’s Hamas-run health ministry.
Heads of state traditionally enjoy diplomatic immunity while in the U.S. on official business. What’s more the U.S. is not a party to the ICC and isn’t legally bound to enforce its warrants. The T

corpus.title: 'Benjamin Netanyahu Will Not Be Arrested While in US': Trump Reacts to Mamdani's Remarks
corpus.signal_text:
'Benjamin Netanyahu Will Not Be Arrested While in US': Trump Reacts to Mamdani's Remarks
The New York City Mayor revealed that municipal officials are engaged in an "active conversation" with the city's Law Department to ascertain the exact scope of local legal authority should the Israeli Prime Minister arrive in the city.
- World News
- 3 min read
Washington: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will "not be arrested while in the United States of America" after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani stated that his administration is examining legal avenues to detain the Israeli leader.
The diplomatic friction intensified after Mamdani asserted to The New York Times that Netanyahu was a "war criminal" who “belongs in The Hague.”
The New York City Mayor revealed that municipal officials are engaged in an "active conversation" with the city's Law Department to ascertain the exact scope of local legal authority should the Israeli Prime Minister arrive in the city.
"Whatever the law allows me to do in New York City, that's what we will do," Mamdani stated, clarifying that his office is not attempting to legislate new local statutes to enforce the measure.
Advertisement
"Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America. He is fighting against the Islamic Republic of Iran, which recently killed 52,000 innocent protestors, and has spent the last 47 years killing American Soldiers, and others. The only ones that should be arrested are the people that led Iran into this unprecedented SPIRAL OF DEATH AND DESTRUCTION, something that should have been dealt with years ago, by previous Presidents! President DONALD J. TRUMP," he wrote.
The Israeli Prime Minister's Office has strongly criticised New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's statement.
Advertisement
The official response, issued on Sunday, dismissed the International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant against Benjamin Netanyahu as "bogus" and labelled the international body a "kangaroo court" that possesses no jurisdiction over either Israel or the United States. The statement further alleged that former ICC prosecutor Karim Khan had initiated the warrant as a diversionary tactic to deflect from subsequent allegations of sexual misconduct against him.
In a sharp rebuke published on X, the Prime Minister's Office asserted that Mamdani was mimicking Khan's strategy by targeting Israel to obscure domestic governa
